name: Pear Tart with Frangipane
recipeIngredient: 1 2-inch piece of vanilla bean, split open
2 strips of lemon zest (1 inch x 2 inches each)
1/2 cup dry or stale macaroons, pulverized
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size, non-reactive saucepan, combine the 1 1/2 cups sugar, split vanilla bean, lemon zest, lemon juice, and 4 cups water. Bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer, and cook, stirring until the sugar is dissolved and the syrup is clear.
2. Peel, cut, seed, core, and stem the pears. Shape half of one pear into a peach-like round by cutting off the stem end. Poach the pear halves in the sugar syrup until tender. Drain on a rack.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. Toast the almonds on a baking sheet until golden brown, then cool and pulverize in a food processor.
4. Heat the milk with the remaining 2-inch piece of vanilla bean.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs, yolks, and 3/4 cup sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Gradually add the flour, stirring constantly. Once absorbed, add the milk, return to heat, and stir until nicely thickened.
6. Pour the pastry cream into a bowl and stir in the butter. Add the almond powder, macaroons, and 1/2 cup kirsch. Allow to cool to room temperature, stirring occasionally; cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate to chill.
7. Heat the apricot jam until clear, then rub through a fine sieve and add the 1/4 cup kirsch. Stir constantly until clear.
8. Spread a thin layer of apricot glaze on the bottom of the baked and cooled pastry shell. Spoon in the frangipane (almond pastry cream) and smooth it out. Set the pear round in the center and arrange the pear halves around it, stem end facing the center. Press gently into the frangipane.
9. Brush the apricot glaze over the pears; sprinkle with pistachios. Refrigerate until 15 minutes before serving (can be made up to one day in advance).
